The [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] '''Type II Orbital Repair Yard''', also known as a '''Type II Orbital Yard''', a '''Type II Orbiting Repair Yard''',<ref name="CTD" /> or a '''type two shipyard''',<ref name="TyT">''[[Tyrant's Test]]''</ref> was a class of [[Space station/Legends|space station]] designed to build and repair [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warship/Legends|warships]].<ref name="CTD" />

==Characteristics==
The Type II design was a square-sided structure, 1,200 meters from side to side and, like most Imperial dock designs, its work bays could be adjusted and rearranged to accommodate [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] of varying sizes and designs, although it usually retained its basic box-shaped configuration. It was designed with 9 [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]],<ref name="CTD" /> and in one configuration, it contained nine bays.<ref name="TyT" />

Like all orbital repair yards, the Type II design was designed not merely to repair ships, but also to construct them, being equipped to build ships from [[Light cruiser/Legends|light cruisers]] upwards.<ref name="CTD" /> Even in its nine-bay configuration, a Type II could be described as being "full of [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]]".<ref name="TyT" />

A Type II had a standard crew of 4,114, and could accommodate 200 troops, typically a defending [[Company/Legends|company]] of Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]]. This garrison, along with powerful shields and armor, compensated for the fact that these stations lacked any real defensive armament. A Class 4 [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] permitted the station to relocate across long distances, although for sublight maneuvers, it required a [[Cargo tug/Legends|cargo tug]].<ref name="CTD" />

==History==
The Type II was one of a number of orbital yard types used throughout the Empire, to support and sometimes replace the [[Deepdock/Legends|deepdocks]] that were the primary shipyards of each [[Sector fleet/Legends|Sector Fleet]]. Whereas deepdocks had a heavy [[Fleet Support Branch]] presence, repair and construction in orbital yards was usually carried out by non-military contractors, often making use of local [[Slavery/Legends|slave labor]].<ref name="CTD" />

The Type II design continued to be important to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] throughout the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. As well as its [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] garrison, each yard was paired with a navy flotilla for protection,<ref name="CTD2">''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]'' describes the defending ships as a "full escort force"; this term could indicate a full [[systems force]], but that force, an absolute minimum of fifty ships and an average of around 100, seems a rather large fleet to tie to the defense of a single 1.2km shipyard; possibly, there was a correlation between the patrol areas of these fleets and the deployment of Type II shipyards.</ref> although after Imperial losses in the final years of the conflict, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] believed that there were no longer enough ships available to provide such dedicated protection.<ref name="CTD" />

Perhaps the most famous Type II yards were those of [[Sixth Negative Regions|Black Sword Command]]. Almost all of the shipyards assigned to this important Imperial fleet were Type II designs,<ref name="CTD" /> with [[Black Nine]] being a typical example,<ref name="TyT" /> although the main [[Black Fifteen]] shipyard at [[N'zoth]] was much larger.<ref name="BtS">''[[Before the Storm]]''</ref> At least eight of the Black Sword shipyards disappeared during the confusion of the Imperial withdrawal from the [[Koornacht Cluster/Legends|Koornacht Cluster]] in [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],<ref name="BFC">The number is based on the following: five Type IIs unaccounted for after the conflict according to ''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]'', plus [[Black Nine]], destroyed at [[ILC-905 system|ILC-905]] in ''[[Tyrant's Test]]'', the unknown shipyard mentioned as having been found and destroyed at [[Tholaz]] in the same novel, and [[Black Eleven (shipyard)|Black Eleven]], which was presumably located after [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]] correctly surmised that it was at [[Doornik-1142/Legends|Doornik-1142]] in ''[[Shield of Lies]]''.</ref> with at least three of them being captured by the [[Yevetha]]ns, who used them to construct the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] fleet that they unleashed in the [[Black Fleet Crisis]] of [[16 ABY/Legends|16]]-[[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]].

The New Republic placed an emphasis on locating and destroying the Yevethan-controlled shipyards,<ref name="SoL">''[[Shield of Lies]]''</ref> leading to engagements like the [[Battle of ILC-905]], where ships from [[Task Force Aster]] found and destroyed [[Black Nine]] and its three defending [[Aramadia-class thrustship|thrustships]] above [[Prildaz]].<ref name="TyT" /> Even so, five of the Type IIs from Black Sword Command remained unaccounted for even after the resolution of the Yevethan conflict, and their absence was a source of some concern to the New Republic.<ref name="CTD" />

==Behind the scenes==
The "type two shipyard" designation was first used in ''[[Tyrant's Test]]'', when [[Black Nine]] was discovered during the [[Battle of ILC-905]]. Black Nine is described in dialogue as being "full of Star Destroyers", and it shares a nine-bay configuration with the large [[Black Fifteen]] shipyard seen at [[N'zoth]] in ''[[Before the Storm]]''.

[[File:ImperialOrbitalShipyard-CrackensThreatDossier.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An Imperial orbital shipyard]]
However, ''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]'' gives the Type II Orbital Yard its canonical size of 1.2km, and also describes it as "cubical", implying that its docks are arranged into a three-dimensional box, rather than a flat grid. These details mean that Black Fifteen, a flat-grid facility capable of holding an [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class ship]], a [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]], a moored [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] and six other warships simultaneously, cannot be identified as a Type II shipyard, and that the majority of Black Sword Command's shipyards were much smaller.

The article which defines the Type II is, however, accompanied by an illustration of a large, flat-grid dock containing ships that resemble a [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] and an [[Interdictor/Legends|interdictor]], both of which may be multiple-kilometer vessels; but as the broad subject of the article is orbital yards in general, this illustration need not represent a Type II facility, and there is no reliable way to scale the ships shown in it, or to identify what class they really are.
